mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware optimizations and potential complexity in setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

pip install kubectl

pip install helm

```

> Install kubectl for managing Kubernetes clusters.
> Install Helm for managing Kubernetes applications.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster on your preferred cloud provider with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to use the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy vLLM to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.17.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.17.0 --namespace vllm --create-namespace

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply your monitoring configuration (ensure you have a valid YAML file).

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove vLLM from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command removes the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up your configurations and data before upgrading. Verify the upgrade after completion.

```bash

kubectl get all -n vllm -o yaml > backup-vllm.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.17.0 --namespace vllm

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Backup current vLLM configurations.
> Upgrade vLLM to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n vllm -o yaml > backup-vllm.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image could not be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the image exists in the specified repository.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' and adjust resource limits if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'FailedScheduling'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources on the cluster to schedule the pod.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has enough resources or adjust the resource requests for the vLLM deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Authentication issues with the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly set up and you have the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods and improved integration with Hugging Face models.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated older quantization methods in favor of new optimizations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.16.0, ensure to update your model configurations to use the new quantization methods.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n vllm' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
